meta.title: G31 Engine Replacement Cost UK — Compare Quotes
meta.description: Every BMW 5 Series G31 engine – B47, B57, B48, B58, N63 and S63. Real UK costs and honest reliability data. Get free quotes from 20+ vetted BMW specialists today.

mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW 5 Series G31 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned G31 engine typically costs £2,000–£15,000 supply only [BMW-QUOTE], depending on the engine – the B47 four-cylinder diesel sits at £2,500–£4,500, while the S63 in the M5 can exceed £15,000. Fitting adds £650–£1,560 at independent specialists. Use our calculator for a personalised figure.

mainEntity.name: Which G31 engine is the most reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The B57D30A (530d) and B58B30 (540i) are both proven, reliable engines – they have no major design flaws beyond routine cooling system and EGR maintenance. The post-LCI N63B44T3 V8 is also significantly improved over the earlier N63. The B47 diesel and B48 petrol are both robust modern four-cylinders.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW 5 Series G31?

acceptedAnswer.text: EGR cooler failure on diesel engines (B47, B57), water pump and cooling system wear on B-series petrol engines (B48, B58), valve stem seals and coolant leaks on the pre-LCI N63 V8 (M550i), and 48V mild-hybrid system issues on LCI models (2020–2023). The quad-turbo B57S in the M550d is complex and rare.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW 5 Series G31 reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes – the G31 is considered one of BMW's most reliable modern generations. The B-series engines are mature and well-understood, with no major catastrophic failure points. The key is maintenance: EGR cooler recalls on diesels, cooling system service on petrols, and regular servicing. The M550i V8 requires more diligent care but is improved from earlier N63 engines.

mainEntity.name: Which G31 engine should I avoid?

acceptedAnswer.text: Pre-LCI N63B44O2 (M550i, 2017–2020) without documented hot-vee service history – valve stem seals and cooling system issues are well-documented. The B57S quad-turbo (M550d) is also complex and rare; parts and specialist knowledge are limited. If budget allows, prioritise post-LCI (2020+) cars.

mainEntity.name: How do I know if my G31 engine needs replacing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Coolant loss without visible leaks (B48/B58), EGR cooler warnings on diesels, persistent oil consumption or blue smoke on startup (N63 V8), or a sudden loss of power are the key warning signs. Use our diagnostic calculator for a personalised assessment.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW 5 Series G31 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Apply the 70% BMW scrap threshold. Against a typical £15,000 520d, a £2,500–£4,500 B47 recon is 17–30% – well within the safe zone. Against a tired £8,000 example, the same recon is 31–56% – still safe but closer to the threshold. For an M5, values are high enough that even a £15,000 S63 replacement is a viable investment.

mainEntity.name: How long do G31 engines last?

acceptedAnswer.text: The B47 and B57 diesel engines are capable of 150,000+ miles with proper maintenance – EGR cooler and AdBlue systems are the main considerations. The B48 and B58 petrol engines are similarly durable, with cooling system maintenance as the key factor. The N63 V8 can achieve 100,000+ miles with diligent servicing, particularly post-LCI units. The S63 in the M5 is robust but demands more frequent maintenance.

mainEntity.name: What is the difference between the G31 and the G30 saloon?

acceptedAnswer.text: The G31 is the Touring (estate) version of the seventh-generation 5 Series, sharing the same platform, engines and interior as the G30 saloon. The G31 offers additional load space and practicality, with a 570-litre boot expanding to 1,700 litres with the rear seats folded. Engine options are identical between the two body styles.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my G31 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate (visible through the windscreen) and vehicle documentation. The engine code is also stamped on the cylinder block – on B-series engines, it's on the left-hand side near the timing cover. Tell us your variant and year (pre-/post-2020 LCI) and we'll identify it – 20+ vetted BMW specialists will confirm it for free.

hero.h1: BMW 5 Series G31 Engines — The Complete UK Guide
hero.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the G31 Touring — from the 2.0-litre B47 diesel and B48 petrol to the 3.0-litre B57 six-cylinder and the M550i's N63 V8. Real UK replacement costs, honest reliability data, and the EGR cooler and cooling system weaknesses that define this generation. Backed by real UK G31 enquiries in 2025 [BMW-VERIFIED].

engineDatabase.h2: BMW 5 Series G31 Engine Codes — The Complete Database
engineDatabase.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the G31 Touring — from the entry-level four-cylinder diesel and petrol to the V8-powered M550i and the quad-turbo M550d.

overview.intro: The BMW G31, launched in February 2017 as the Touring (estate) version of the seventh-generation 5 Series, combines the practicality of a large estate with the dynamic prowess expected of a BMW. Built on the CLAR (Cluster Architecture) platform shared with the G30 saloon and G11 7 Series, the G31 features a 40:60 front-to-rear weight distribution and a range of turbocharged four-, six- and eight-cylinder engines. A 2020 facelift brought 48V mild-hybrid technology, updated styling and the introduction of the 545e plug-in hybrid.
overview.keyFacts: Platform: BMW CLAR (G30/G31) • Predecessor: BMW 5 Series F11 • Successor: BMW 5 Series G61 (2023–present) • Sibling body styles in this generation: G30 (saloon), G32 (6 Series Gran Turismo)
overview.marketIntelligenceLine: The G31 generated real UK engine enquiries in 2025 [BMW-VERIFIED], with the B47 diesel and B48 petrol accounting for the majority of demand as these cars enter their second and third ownership cycles.

items.engine: B57D30A (530d) – "The 3.0-litre straight-six diesel is BMW's finest modern diesel – 265 hp of smooth, effortless torque with none of the timing chain anxiety of the earlier N57. EGR cooler and AdBlue are the only real demands; this engine is widely considered bulletproof."
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want six-cylinder diesel refinement without the quad-turbo complexity.

items.engine: B58B30C (540i xDrive, post-LCI) – "The B58 straight-six is BMW's finest modern petrol engine – 333 hp of silky-smooth power with proven reliability. The 48V mild-hybrid system smooths stop-start and boosts off-line response. Water pump and cooling system are the only real demands."
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want effortless six-cylinder power with modern reliability.

items.engine: N63B44O2 (M550i, pre-LCI) – "The early N63 V8 carries the hot-vee design's heat-related weaknesses – valve stem seals, coolant hoses and battery drain issues are well-documented. The post-LCI N63B44T3 is significantly improved."
items.whoItsFor: only buyers who accept V8 maintenance costs and have checked the car's service history carefully.

items.engine: S63B44T4 (M5) – "The high-output V8 is a masterpiece of engineering, but any internal failure – turbocharger, timing chain or valvetrain – can run to £15,000+. This is not an engine for the faint-hearted."
items.whoItsFor: buyers with deep pockets and full service history.

items.engine: B47D20A (520d) – "The 2.0-litre diesel delivers 190 hp with excellent economy and proven B-series reliability. No timing chain anxiety of the earlier N47 – just solid, dependable motoring. It's the G31's volume seller for good reason."
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want G31 estate practicality with minimal running costs.

items.engine: B58B30C + EM (545e xDrive) – "The plug-in hybrid combines the B58 six-cylinder with an electric motor for 394 hp and genuine electric-only running. Most buyers default to diesel or V8, but the 545e is the smart long-term choice for urban users who want performance and efficiency."
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want six-cylinder performance with hybrid efficiency.

rows.ownershipVerdict: Watch – hybrid complexity; warranty is crucial.
Best Petrol – BMW's finest modern six.
Specialist – six-cylinder hybrid; complex but rewarding.
Watch – hot-vee V8; check history carefully.
Solid – improved V8, still premium running costs.

economicsRule.title: The G31 rule
economicsRule.text: A £2,500–£4,500 B47 recon against a £10,000–£20,000 G31 sits at 13–45% of the car's value – a routine repair decision for most examples. Even the most expensive M5 replacement at £15,000 is under 30% of a typical £50,000+ example. The BMW 70% threshold is rarely triggered on the G31 – values are high enough to absorb even significant engine bills. That said, always factor in the car's condition and service history; a neglected example may not be worth the investment.

keyTakeaways.question: Which G31 Costs Least To Own?
keyTakeaways.answer: 520d (B47) – cheapest entry, lowest insurance, and proven diesel economy.

keyTakeaways.question: Which G31 Costs Most To Own?
keyTakeaways.answer: M5 (S63) – high-output V8 with premium running costs and specialist maintenance.

keyTakeaways.question: Which G31 Is Best Value?
keyTakeaways.answer: 530d (B57) – the sweet spot between performance and running costs.

problems.title: EGR Cooler Failure (Diesel Engines)
problems.affectedModels: G31 diesel variants (B47, B57, B57S), particularly pre-2020 examples.
problems.rootCause: The EGR cooler can develop internal coolant leaks, leading to coolant loss, white smoke and potential engine damage. BMW has issued recalls for some variants.
problems.recommendation: Check if your G31 diesel is covered by the EGR cooler recall – if not, have the cooler inspected at every service. A £400–£1,000 repair is far cheaper than the £2,500+ replacement that follows an overheat.

cta.label: Compare EGR cooler repair quotes for your BMW 5 Series G31 →

problems.title: B48/B58 Water Pump & Cooling System
problems.affectedModels: G31 petrol variants with B48 or B58 engines (520i, 530i, 540i).
problems.rootCause: The plastic water pump impeller and thermostat housing can degrade with heat cycling – a known characteristic of BMW's B-series engines. Coolant leaks are the first sign.
problems.recommendation: At the first sign of coolant loss, have the water pump and thermostat housing inspected – a £300–£600 repair is cheap insurance against a £2,000+ replacement. Replace the coolant at the same time.

cta.label: Compare B48/B58 engine prices for your BMW 5 Series G31 →

problems.title: N63 V8 Valve Stem Seals & Coolant Leaks (Pre-LCI M550i)
problems.rootCause: The hot-vee V8 design generates significant heat – valve stem seals can harden and leak, causing oil consumption and blue smoke on startup. Coolant hoses and plastic fittings near the turbochargers also degrade.
problems.recommendation: If you own a pre-LCI M550i, monitor oil consumption carefully and have the cooling system inspected annually. A £1,000–£3,000 valve stem seal replacement is far cheaper than the £6,000+ replacement that follows a catastrophic failure. Post-LCI N63B44T3 cars have improved seals and cooling system components.

cta.label: Compare N63 engine prices for your BMW 5 Series G31 →

problems.title: 48V Mild-Hybrid Battery & System Issues (LCI Models)
problems.affectedModels: G31 LCI models with 48V mild-hybrid system (2020–2023, most petrol and diesel variants).
problems.rootCause: The 48V lithium-ion battery and associated electronics can develop issues – symptoms include reduced stop-start functionality, warning lights and, in rare cases, complete system failure.
problems.recommendation: Have the 48V battery health checked at every major service – a £300–£600 repair or software update is far cheaper than a £1,800+ battery replacement. Keep the car on a trickle charger if left unused for extended periods.

cta.label: Compare 48V hybrid repair quotes for your BMW 5 Series G31 →

replacementCosts.h2: G31 Engine Replacement Costs — By Engine and Condition

coreVariants.scopeNote: All variants listed are G31 only. The G30 saloon and G32 6 Series Gran Turismo share most of these engines but are covered on their own Generation Pages. The M5 (F90) is technically a separate model but shares the G30/G31 platform; its engine is included for completeness. The M550d xDrive with the B57S quad-turbo diesel was discontinued after 2020.

mostRequestedEngines: 1. B47D20A/B (520d) – diesel enquiries dominate the volume
mostCommonFailures: 3. N63 V8 valve stem seals (pre-LCI M550i)
